text: The surgeon isolates and sections the right natal ureter, followed by the isolation and sectioning of the right renal graft pedicle. The right graft ureter is isolated up to the bladder. Despite the patient having a right solitary kidney, the left native ureter is identified. The left graft filter is isolated with careful attention to preserve its integrity and maximize its length.

The surgeon performs bilateral intrafascial nerve sparing. Subsequently, the membranous urethra is isolated and sectioned, resulting in the removal of all urothelium from the bladder, native urethra, and right renal graft.

The surgeon constructs the Florence robotic intracorporeal neobladder. Posterior reconstruction is performed between the Denonvilliers' fascia and the peritoneum using a 3-0 barbed suture. The urethroneal bladder anastomosis is completed with two running hemisutures. The neobladder is de-tubularized and reconfigured following the flooring technique.
The surgeon performs a single urethral neobladder anastomosis, connecting the neobladder to the left graft urethra.
